relations.note: Adjacency enforcement relies on the declared background color in section metadata.
Both govern how self-contained blocks stack, including against locked components.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.rule_text: All colors in code must be expressed as HEX; all sizing in px; use inline CSS only for email-client compatibility; and duplicate `bgcolor` attributes for Outlook.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.

Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Enable safe modular stacking of independent section blocks.
rules.rule_text: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px (size.email.body_width_600 = 600px) with its own top/bottom 32px padding (padding.section.vertical_32 = 32px) and a declared background color; sections carry no margins outside the block so assembly is direct stacking. Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

quotes: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time**

Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Avoid footnote symbol collisions across independently authored sections.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols are superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time; sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Prevent broken decorative continuity against team-inserted locked blocks.
rules.rule_text: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

effect.reserved_for: non-adjacent placement (not next to another dark section)
effect.subject: dark_background_section

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections
Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Maintain visual rhythm and section separation.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).
